title: fraxinus americana subsp. typicum
intro: Fraxinus americana subsp. typicum, also known as the white ash, is a subspecies of the white ash tree, native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive white bark and pinnate leaves. The bark and leaves of the Fraxinus americana subsp. typicum have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana subsp. typicum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus americana subsp. typicum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
